note: Public receipts ledger. Every entry is committed before the outcome is known; graded entries record the pre-registered probability, the outcome, hit/miss on the modal call, and the Brier score (lower is better; 0.25 = coin flip). This file is the source of truth for the website receipts page.

validation.description: v2 model frozen on pre-June data, scored on 884 unseen games (2026-06-01 through 2026-07-11).

entries.title: MLP Mid-Season Tournament Gold Final — New Jersey 5s vs St. Louis Shock
entries.outcome_summary: St. Louis Shock won 3-0. MXD2 skipped as a dead game; no DreamBreaker. Anna Leigh Waters lost both her games (6-11 WD, 8-11 MXD1).

items.label: Overall: St. Louis Shock wins the final
WD: Waters/Johnson over Bright/Fahey
MD: Tardio/Patriquin over Khlif/Howells
MXD1: Waters/Khlif over Bright/Patriquin
MXD2: Fahey/Tardio over Johnson/Howells
Match reaches a DreamBreaker

entries.footnote: The earlier v1 forecast (same file, committed hours before) grades identically in direction: overall STL 57.0% HIT, WD 69.5% MISS, MD 79.1% HIT, MXD1 52.9% MISS. Both models called the match and missed the headline WD game.

entries.title: Preregistered chemistry predictions (second half of 2026 season)
entries.model: v1 dyad chemistry + fixed-effects regression scoring (method frozen in the source file)

items.label: Waters + Bright (womens, PPA) underperform sum-of-parts in remaining 2026 games
Alshon + Patriquin (mens, PPA) underperform sum-of-parts in remaining 2026 games
Bright + Patriquin (mixed) chemistry stays small: |chem| < 1 point/game unshrunk
League-wide dyad-chemistry scale (sd_d) refit on H2-only games stays under 1.0 point/game

entries.footnote: Scored in September against games dated after 2026-07-12 only, using the method written into the frozen file.

entries.title: MLP San Diego day 1 — five-matchup slate vs the frozen site forecasts
entries.source: data/forecasts.json (generated 2026-07-14, committed to main before the event)
entries.model: v2 current values, projected lineups, singles-model DreamBreakers, calibrated
entries.outcome_summary: Favorites went 4/5. Columbus swept both its matchups 4-0; SoCal and Phoenix won as modest favorites; the miss was Phoenix-Orlando, where 61% Phoenix lost 2-3 on a DreamBreaker the model had priced at 37%. Jack Sock (in for the projected Yates Johnson) played both Orlando matchups.

items.label: Matchup 1: Columbus Sliders over Orlando Squeeze
Matchup 2: SoCal Hard Eights over Florida Smash
Matchup 3: Phoenix Flames over Orlando Squeeze
Matchup 4: Columbus Sliders over California Black Bears
Matchup 5: Phoenix Flames over Florida Smash

entries.footnote: Matchup mean Brier 0.130 (coin flip = 0.25). Game level, matched on the forecast's own team-one pairing: 12/13 hits, mean Brier 0.092, two modal scores exact (both 11-6 mixed calls); 7 games VOID where the projected pairing never took the court, and opponent-side substitutions (Sock for Johnson, twice) graded through as lineup risk. Reaches-a-DreamBreaker graded 4/5 on the modal call, mean Brier 0.136 — the miss was the 37.4% DB that decided Phoenix-Orlando. POST-HOC COUNTERFACTUAL (not a regrade; committed grades stand): repricing the four Sock-affected games with actual lineups (Sock 0.837 vs Johnson 0.369 on 448/303 games) flips the matchup-3 call to Orlando 68% (Brier 0.377 → 0.102) and dissolves the MD game miss (0.316 → 0.023) — the day's headline miss was lineup information, not skill values. It cuts the other way too: Columbus's wins over the Sock-priced pairs (11-6, 11-1) become 48% and 90% calls, so the affected games' Brier sum rises 0.362 → 0.507. The model can't take credit both ways; the ledger keeps the frozen numbers.

entries.title: Pre-registered momentum tests on 2,500 matches of rally logs
entries.source: model/momentum_prereg.md (hypotheses, estimators, decision criteria, and analysis code model/momentum_test.py frozen before any sequential statistic was computed); results in model/momentum_results.md
entries.model: cluster-robust logistic regressions on 191,500 resolved rallies (2,500 matches, 0 dropped); serve state + v2 strength controls; alpha .01 + practical thresholds
entries.outcome_summary: Momentum is not detectable in pro pickleball rallies. Within-possession streaks +0.11pp (99% CI [-0.72, +0.94], bounded null), trailing-5-rally momentum +0.17pp ([-0.19, +0.52], bounded null), game-point pressure -0.01pp ([-1.31, +1.29], bounded null). Timeouts: inconclusive, direction mildly AGAINST the caller (+1.53pp for the opponent's next serve rally, p=.014, CI reaches +3.1pp).

items.label: H1: no meaningful within-possession momentum (|APE| < 1pp or p >= .01)
H2: no meaningful cross-possession momentum (trailing-5 window)
H3: no meaningful receiving-side timeout effect (|APE| < 2pp)
items.outcome: +1.53pp, p=.014 (not sig at .01); CI [-0.07, +3.12] exceeds +2pp so not bounded — no effect established, formally inconclusive

items.label: H4: no meaningful game-point pressure effect (|APE| < 2pp)
items.outcome: -0.01pp, 99% CI [-1.31, +1.29] — servers convert game points at exactly their usual rate, BOUNDED

entries.footnote: 4/4 registered predictions hit, mean Brier 0.044. Secondaries: lag-1 rally -0.18pp (ns); serving-side timeouts -3.24pp on only 512 events (ns, CI +/-5.5pp). Descriptive bonus: timeouts are called by the RECEIVING side 12.5x as often as the serving side (6,417 vs 512) - they are momentum-stopping attempts by the team being scored on, and the next-rally evidence says they do not detectably work. Caveats as registered: logged matches are not a random sample of events, and the timeout estimate admits a selection interpretation; under the iid null the tests remain exact.

entries.title: ADDENDUM H3b: windowed timeout effect (next-10-rally differential)
entries.source: model/momentum_prereg.md ADDENDUM H3b + model/momentum_h3b.py (frozen before any windowed post-timeout statistic was computed)
entries.model: OLS with cluster-robust SEs: next-10-rally point differential, receiving-side timeout vs untreated moments, adjusted for pre-window differential, margin, server number, strength
entries.outcome_summary: No meaningful windowed timeout effect: theta = +0.060 points per 10-rally window (99% CI [-0.034, +0.155], p=0.10) - BOUNDED under the 0.5-point threshold. Secondary 5-rally window +0.044 [-0.017, +0.105]. The trigger profile confirms the intuition about WHEN timeouts happen (called after an average -2.6-point run vs -0.25 at ordinary moments), and the naive after-minus-before comparison shows +2.30 points of apparent improvement - almost exactly the +2.06 a zero-effect simulation with endogenous calling produces. The perceived timeout turnaround is regression to the mean.

items.label: H3b: no meaningful windowed timeout effect (|theta| < 0.5 pts per 10 rallies or p >= .01)
items.outcome: +0.060 pts/10 rallies, 99% CI [-0.034, +0.155] - no meaningful effect, BOUNDED

entries.footnote: User-motivated refinement of H3 (strategic adjustments may express over several rallies, not one). The naive before-vs-after comparison is demonstrated to be biased under the null by the endogenous-timeout selftest; the registered comparison is treated vs untreated moments of the same kind.

entries.title: ADDENDUM H3c: the rally-share steelman (final timeout test on this sample)
entries.source: model/momentum_prereg.md ADDENDUM H3c + model/momentum_h3c.py (frozen before any rally-share post-timeout statistic was computed)
entries.model: Same matched-moment design as H3b; outcome = receiver's share of next 10 rallies won. Secondaries: 3-rally share, possession-killer, severe-trigger subset. Any positive requires disjoint-sample replication before any claim.
entries.outcome_summary: Bounded null at the steelman's strongest: receiving side wins +0.20pp more of the next 10 rallies after its timeout (99% CI [-0.30, +0.71], p=.31; threshold was 2.5pp). Possession-killer dead null (-0.05pp); severe-trigger subset -0.11pp; 3-rally share +0.86pp at p=.045 (lone sub-threshold secondary; noise per the registered language). The naive before/after illusion SCALES with desperation - +16.4pp overall, +25.5pp for severe triggers - while the adjusted effect stays at zero: the more the timeout was needed, the bigger the regression-to-the-mean mirage. Timeout program closed on this sample; replication pool reserved but nothing to replicate.

items.label: H3c: no meaningful rally-share timeout effect (|theta| < 2.5pp or p >= .01)
items.outcome: +0.20pp of next-10-rally share, 99% CI [-0.30, +0.71] - no meaningful effect, BOUNDED

entries.footnote: User-proposed steelman: rallies are the proximal outcome (killing the server's possession precedes scoring, which point differentials cannot see until converted). Declared the FINAL timeout estimand on this sample; replication pool (untouched ~10k eligible matches, seed 20260718) reserved for any positive.

entries.title: ADDENDUM H3d (amended): window-profile confirmation of the timeout question
entries.source: model/momentum_prereg.md ADDENDUM H3d + model/momentum_h3d.py (frozen before any statistic touched the replication pool)
entries.model: Window profile theta_W for W in {1,2,3,4,5,7,10} on the full untouched 9,426-match pool, identical moments/controls. Primary: theta_3 > 0 one-sided alpha .01. Family: other windows at Bonferroni |z|>=2.69. Bounding: all seven 99% CIs vs +/-2.5pp. Amended pre-unblinding (user's argument: one cell cannot close a family for a skeptical reader in either direction).

items.label: H3d primary: the 3-rally hint FAILS to replicate (one-sided p >= .01)
H3d family: no window in {1,2,4,5,7,10} clears Bonferroni |z|>=2.69

entries.footnote: Hypothesis generated on sample A (lone p=.045 secondary among ~10 estimands), tested once on disjoint data. Even a successful replication claims only a real-but-tiny sub-1pp huddle effect, far below the 2.5pp meaningful bar.
